steps: The arborist estimates at least 50 and no more than 60 hickory trees per acre, so the density satisfies \(50 \leq \text{trees per acre} \leq 60\).
For a 3-acre section, multiply every part of the inequality by 3: \(50 \times 3 = 150\) and \(60 \times 3 = 180\), giving \(150 \leq x \leq 180\).

Evaluate all four choices:
  • A: \(50 \leq x \leq 60\) — these are the per-acre bounds, not scaled to 3 acres. Eliminated.
  • B: \(150 \leq x \leq 180\) — lower bound \(50 \times 3 = 150\), upper bound \(60 \times 3 = 180\). That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: \(50 \leq x \leq 180\) — lower bound is unscaled while upper bound is scaled; inconsistent. Eliminated.
  • D: \(150 \leq x \leq 60\) — lower bound is greater than upper bound, which is impossible. Eliminated.
